title: Jerk chicken
subtitle: Jamaican spice-marinated chicken, blistered and smoky.
excerpt: Chicken pieces marinated overnight in a blend of scotch bonnet, allspice, thyme and soy sauce, then roasted or grilled until charred and intensely flavoured. The signature dish of Jamaica. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Jerk chicken. Jerk seasoning originates with the Maroons of Jamaica, enslaved Africans who escaped into the Blue Mountains and developed the cooking technique using the island's pimento (allspice) tree. The dish is documented in Jamaican culinary history from the 18th century onward. Levi Roots's Reggae Reggae cookbook provides a modern reference.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Marinate overnight; the longer the marinade, the better the result.

content.text: Jerk is one of the most aromatic spice blends in the world: scotch bonnet for heat, allspice for warmth, fresh thyme for fragrance, spring onion and garlic for depth. The overnight marinade is not optional — it takes time for the scotch bonnet's fruity heat to penetrate the meat. Score the chicken deeply before marinating.
Scotch bonnet is very hot. Handle with gloves and do not touch your face. Adjust the quantity to your heat tolerance; even half a scotch bonnet gives significant heat.

items.prepNote: or 1 for less heat; deseeded for milder

content.text: Blend {{scotch-bonnet}} of scotch bonnets, {{spring-onion}} of spring onions, {{garlic}} of garlic, {{ginger-root}} of ginger, {{thyme}} of thyme leaves, {{allspice}} of allspice, {{cinnamon-ground}} of cinnamon, {{nutmeg-ground}} of nutmeg, {{dark-brown-sugar}} of brown sugar, {{soy-sauce-dark}} of soy sauce, the juice of {{lime}} lime, {{rapeseed-oil}} of oil, {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt and {{black-pepper}} of pepper in a food processor to a rough paste. Score the chicken thighs deeply with a knife. Rub the marinade over every surface and into the scores. Cover and refrigerate overnight.

Heat the oven to 220°C. Lay the chicken on a lined baking tray. Roast for 40 to 45 minutes until the skin is deeply charred and the meat is cooked through, with an internal temperature of 74°C. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

items.cause: Chicken not scored, or marinated for less than 4 hours.
items.fix: Score deeply to the bone in several places. The marinade needs time and access to penetrate. Overnight is standard.
The heat in scotch bonnet comes from the flesh and seeds. Remove the seeds for a milder result, but leave the white membrane for more.

items.cause: Overcooked, or breast pieces instead of thighs.
items.fix: Use bone-in thighs. Check temperature at 40 minutes; 74°C is fully cooked. A few degrees above this and the meat will be dry.

content.text: Jerk chicken belongs to the roadside stalls and backyard grills of Jamaica, cooked over pimento wood in a halved oil drum. The smoke from the wood is part of the dish's traditional flavour; at home, the oven gives the char and the marinade gives the rest. Serve with rice and peas, plantain or festival. The marinade also works brilliantly on pork ribs.
